title: Fluffy Yeasty Mexican Pork Tacos
description: A rich, yeasty, and fluffy Mexican (Oaxacan) dish featuring pork back ribs, air-fried to perfection. Served in a date-night style with an Ayurvedic and Tamasic-friendly twist.

ingredients.name: Active dry yeast
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality yeast for better flavor.

ingredients.name: Warm water
ingredients.notes: Use filtered water for best results.

ingredients.name: Flour
ingredients.notes: Use unbleached all-purpose flour for a lighter crust.

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cumin for a bolder flavor.

ingredients.name: Coriander
ingredients.notes: Coriander is a key spice in Oaxacan cuisine.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Use fresh cilantro for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Use fresh lime juice for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Avocado oil
ingredients.notes: Use a neutral-tasting oil for frying.

ingredients.name: Tortillas
ingredients.notes: Use corn tortillas for a more authentic experience.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the yeast, sugar, and warm water. Stir gently to dissolve the yeast, then let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add the flour, cumin, coriander, and salt to the bowl. Mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour until doubled in size.
Preheat the air fryer basket to 400°F (200°C).
Cut the pork back ribs into 2-inch pieces. Toss with avocado oil, cumin, and coriander until evenly coated.
Place the pork in the air fryer basket and cook for 10 minutes until crispy and golden brown.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the tacos by placing a few pieces of pork onto a warmed tortilla, followed by a spoonful of the yeasty dough, a sprinkle of cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice.
